title: Festivals & events
subtitle: 42 celebrations through the year — pick a day to see what's on.

festivals.name: New Year's Day
festivals.description: Denmark's official start to the year celebrated nationwide with family gatherings, meals, and reflections on the year ahead.

festivals.name: CPH PIX Film Festival
festivals.description: A major international film festival showcasing documentaries, shorts, and features in Copenhagen's cinemas.

festivals.name: Winter Jazz Festival
festivals.description: Northern Jutland's celebration of jazz music with performances across venues in Aalborg and surrounding towns.

festivals.name: Carnival in Aalborg
festivals.description: Colorful pre-Lenten festivities with parades, costumes, and street parties celebrating the season in Denmark's north.

festivals.name: Mardi Gras in Aalborg
festivals.description: One of Denmark's most vibrant carnival celebrations featuring masked balls, street performances, and elaborate costumes.

festivals.name: Maundy Thursday
festivals.description: Thursday before Easter when children dress as witches and exchange drawings for candy in a Danish Easter tradition.

festivals.name: Good Friday
festivals.description: A national public holiday marking the crucifixion of Jesus, observed with church services and family time.

festivals.name: Easter Sunday & Monday
festivals.description: Denmark's beloved Easter celebration featuring egg hunts, chocolate, and family gatherings across the country.

festivals.name: Aalborg Carnival
festivals.description: A four-day celebration of music, theater, and street art that transforms Aalborg into a vibrant cultural hub.

festivals.name: Great Prayer Day
festivals.description: A uniquely Danish public holiday falling between Easter and Ascension, combining religious and national traditions.

festivals.name: Ascension Day
festivals.description: A Christian holiday marking Jesus's ascension to heaven, observed as a public holiday in Denmark.

festivals.name: Whit Sunday & Monday
festivals.description: Pentecost celebration marking the Holy Spirit's descent, observed with church services and family outings.

festivals.name: Copenhagen Marathon
festivals.description: A major spring marathon event attracting thousands of runners through Copenhagen's streets and parks.

festivals.name: Roskilde Festival
festivals.description: One of Europe's largest music festivals featuring rock, indie, and alternative bands in a historic setting near Copenhagen.

festivals.name: Copenhagen Jazz Festival
festivals.description: A world-renowned jazz festival transforming Copenhagen with performances in clubs, streets, and concert halls.

festivals.name: Tønder Festival
festivals.description: A prestigious folk music and world music festival in historic Tønder celebrating diverse musical traditions.

festivals.name: Skanderborg Festival
festivals.description: A four-day rock, pop, and indie music festival in Skanderborg drawing diverse audiences and international acts.

festivals.name: Bornholm Jazz Festival
festivals.description: An intimate jazz festival celebrating the island's vibrant music scene with performances in unique venues.

festivals.name: Aarhus Festival
festivals.description: Denmark's largest cultural festival featuring theater, music, food, and art across Denmark's second-largest city.

festivals.name: Odense Flower Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of flowers and gardens in Hans Christian Andersen's hometown featuring displays and events.

festivals.name: Smørrebrød Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of Denmark's iconic open-faced sandwich featuring tastings and culinary demonstrations.

festivals.name: Frederiksborg Festival
festivals.description: A baroque music and cultural festival held in Frederiksborg Castle, one of Scandinavia's most significant monuments.

festivals.name: Fårup Sommerland's Summer Festival
festivals.description: A family-friendly festival at the amusement park with live music, entertainment, and summer activities.

festivals.name: Copenhagen Cooking
festivals.description: A prestigious food festival celebrating Nordic cuisine, featuring chef demonstrations and culinary experiences.

festivals.name: Nørrebro Festival
festivals.description: A vibrant neighborhood festival in Copenhagen's trendy Nørrebro district with music, food, and cultural activities.

festivals.name: Stevns Klint Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of Denmark's dramatic chalk cliffs with performances, exhibitions, and nature-focused activities.

festivals.name: Odense Marathon
festivals.description: A spring running event through the charming streets of Hans Christian Andersen's birthplace.

festivals.name: Ringsted Medieval Festival
festivals.description: A historical reenactment festival celebrating medieval times with performances, crafts, and entertainment.

festivals.name: Svendborg Summer Festival
festivals.description: A seaside festival in the charming harbor town with live music, theater, and maritime celebrations.

festivals.name: Ribe Festival
festivals.description: A cultural festival in Denmark's oldest town featuring medieval history, crafts, and traditional entertainment.

festivals.name: Lolland-Falster Food Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of local agricultural products and regional cuisine in Denmark's southern islands.

festivals.name: Kolding Troldesalen Festival
festivals.description: A festival celebrating theater and performing arts in the charming medieval town of Kolding.

festivals.name: Velje Street Food Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of international and local street food in the vibrant city of Vejle.

festivals.name: Moesgård Museum Festival
festivals.description: An archaeological and cultural festival at Denmark's prehistoric open-air museum near Aarhus.

festivals.name: Viborg Festival
festivals.description: A multi-genre festival in central Jutland featuring music, theater, and community celebrations.

festivals.name: Gilleleje Fishing Festival
festivals.description: A seaside celebration of fishing culture and maritime traditions in North Zealand's charming harbor town.

festivals.name: Constitution Day
festivals.description: Denmark's official celebration of the signing of the Danish Constitution in 1849, featuring parades and flag displays.

festivals.name: Midsummer's Eve (Sankt Hans Aften)
festivals.description: Denmark's beloved summer solstice celebration featuring bonfires, singing, and witch burning traditions.

festivals.name: Bornholm Art Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of visual arts and craftsmanship on the beautiful island of Bornholm.

festivals.name: Holstebro International Theatre Festival
festivals.description: A prestigious biennial theater festival showcasing international and experimental performances.

festivals.name: Christmas Markets and Winter Festivities
festivals.description: Copenhagen and towns nationwide transform with festive markets, holiday lights, and traditional Danish Christmas celebrations.

festivals.name: Christmas Day and Boxing Day
festivals.description: Denmark's cherished Christmas celebration featuring family gatherings, traditional food, and holiday traditions.
